title: Threshold Chalk
desc: The ward marks at the first inner door have worn thin again, which nobody here is willing to call trivial.

steps.text:
The first inner door of Stone Pillar is less a door than an agreement. The outer crews maintain the threshold, the inner corridors remain unentered without cause, and the chalk marks across the lintel tell everyone exactly where the bargain begins. The marks are not presented as protection by anyone respectable. They are presented as protocol, which is the same thing with less room for panic in the phrasing. Overnight damp has blurred half the line.

The ward clerk uncaps a chalk case and gestures to the stone. "Renew the lintel marks if you want the upper line," she says. "Or replace the ash trays at the door base if you prefer the floor. We do not claim the signs do anything mystical. We claim they tell us quickly when something has changed."

options.label: Renew the chalk marks across the lintel and jambs
Replace the ash trays at the door base

steps.text:
The lintel marks are short angled strokes and circles drawn at measured intervals across the stone frame so any disturbance stands out immediately against the known pattern. You wipe away the blurred remnants, redraw each mark to the clerk's guide notches, and step back often to make sure the sequence reads cleanly from both sides of the hall. Once restored, the threshold looks watched again instead of merely old.

The clerk studies the completed line with a severity that suggests poor chalk work is only one step below treason. This time, she seems satisfied. "Good. Now if the door chooses to become interesting, we will at least know where it began."

options.label: Cap the chalk and leave the threshold legible again

steps.text:
The ash trays sit in shallow recesses at the base of the door to catch any draft, grit, or powder carried out from the inner corridor. Most days they gather almost nothing, which is how everyone prefers them. Today two trays are cracked and a third has been left full long enough to become ambiguous. You clear the old ash, seat the fresh trays into their recesses, and level each one so the next disturbance will leave an honest record instead of a muddled guess.

The clerk taps the nearest tray lightly and nods when the ash settles smooth across its surface. "Better. Evidence is most useful when the container has not already started lying."

options.label: Stack the cracked trays aside and leave the door properly watched
